title: centaurea pectinata subsp. supina
intro: Centaurea pectinata subsp. supina, also known as a type of knapweed, is a perennial plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Centaurea pectinata subsp. supina can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
